droppedFalsePositives.reason: Declared as CSS var --primary on GoHighLevel funnel template but never used on rendered pages. Not in logo, navbar, or any visible element.
Generic Tailwind-ish gray from body text color — a neutral, not a brand color.
One-off contact form field border color — not a brand color.

contrastIssues: #188BF6 button with white text = ~3.9:1 — fails WCAG AA for normal text but passes for large text (18px+ bold or 24px+). Fine for CTA buttons, not fine for inline body links on light backgrounds. Fix: use #0E5DC0 as the text-on-light variant (already present in their palette).
colorAudit.harmonyType: monochromatic-cool (navy + electric blue are within same hue family)
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: strong — one electric accent, dark substrate, white text. Classic performance-brand palette.
colorAudit.saturationNotes: #188BF6 is highly saturated (~90%) — works perfectly as the 'one electric accent' in performance-athletic style. Keep as-is.
colorAudit.backgroundAssessment: Current site alternates pure black (#000) and deep navy (#001C53) as section backgrounds. Too much contrast jump between sections — unified obsidian substrate reads more premium.

colorShifts: Unified section backgrounds from two values (#000 + #001C53) to one obsidian substrate #0A0E1A — eliminates the jarring contrast jump between sections and gives the site a calmer, more premium read.
Added --bg-card #141929 and --bg-elevated #1C2338 for proper surface hierarchy (instead of flat one-color sections).
Kept --accent exactly as their signature #188BF6 — this is their identity and performance-athletic uses one electric accent.
Added --accent-glow (low-opacity blue) for CTA halo / hover illumination effects used in performance-athletic style.
text-primary shifted from pure #FFFFFF to #F5F7FA (slight cool tint) — easier on the eyes on dark substrate, matches the cool tone of the accent.

fontRecommendation.display: Archivo Black (900) or Space Grotesk (700) — bold athletic grotesk for all-caps headers and kinetic marquees
fontRecommendation.body: Inter (400–600) — clean, legible sans for body copy and schedule tables
fontRecommendation.accent: JetBrains Mono or IBM Plex Mono for eyebrow labels, class times, and data callouts
fontRecommendation.reasoning: Performance-athletic style demands bold, heavy-weight display type for its kinetic energy. Archivo Black / Space Grotesk 700 gives the marquee + hero type the sportswear-brand weight it needs. Inter handles body copy cleanly at every size including the schedule grid. A mono accent for class times and labels adds the data-driven performance cue without going full training-lab.
